_note: Feature tables for the dark fantasy classes. n = feature name, d = one-line rules text the DM treats as authoritative.

1.d: Bonus action: brand a creature you can see; your attacks against it deal +1d6 radiant until it dies or you rest.

2.d: Casts paladin-list spells as litanies using WIS, spoken through clenched teeth.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: Action: learn whether a creature within 30 ft is a fiend, undead, shapechanger, or under a curse or charm.

7.d: 1/rest, your Hunter's Brand target takes 2d10 radiant when you hit it (declared before the roll).

9.d: Advantage on saves against charm and possession; allies within 10 ft add +1d4 against fear.

10.d: Your branded quarry cannot hide from you; you know its direction within 1 mile.

11.d: Maintain two Hunter's Brands at once; brand damage rises to +1d8.

13.d: 1/rest, a ranged attack against your brand becomes an automatic critical if it hits.

14.d: Resistance to necrotic damage; curses targeting you have disadvantage.

15.d: Concentration on your litanies cannot be broken by damage of 20 or less.

17.d: Your critical hits against branded creatures prevent regeneration and revival for 24 hours.

18.d: 1/long rest, take an extra full turn immediately after killing your branded quarry.

20.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute all your attacks against fiends, undead, and cursed creatures deal maximum weapon dice.

3.d: Your ranged attacks count as silvered and magical.
1/rest, emit bright warding light 20 ft for 10 minutes; hostile undead and fiends in it have disadvantage on attacks against your allies.

7.d: 1/rest, when a fiend or undead hits you, it takes 3d8 radiant (CON save halves).

11.d: Allies within 20 ft deal +1d4 radiant with weapon attacks against your branded quarry.

15.d: 1/long rest, mass brand: every enemy within 30 ft is branded at once (WIS save negates each).

1.d: You and allies within 5 ft have resistance to poison damage and advantage against disease.
After observing a creature for a round, learn one vulnerability or resistance it has.

2.d: Casts cleric-list spells as remedies and toxins using INT; the kit and mask are the focus.

5.d: 1/rest, a melee hit also deals 2d8 necrotic and the target cannot regain HP until your next turn.

6.d: Stabilize as a bonus action; your stabilized patients wake with 1d4 HP after 1 minute.

7.d: Immune to inhaled poisons and gases; advantage on saves against fear while masked.

9.d: Your healing remedies also cure one disease or poison per casting.

10.d: Examining a body reveals cause, time, and manner of death precisely, including magical causes.

11.d: Lance the Wound damage rises to 4d8 and spreads: one creature adjacent to the target takes half.

13.d: 1/long rest, a 20-ft radius circle for 10 minutes: enemies treat it as difficult terrain and take 2d6 poison entering (CON save halves).

15.d: Miasma Ward radius grows to 15 ft and also grants resistance to necrotic damage.

17.d: Your Anatomist's Eye reveals all vulnerabilities and resistances at a glance.

18.d: 1/long rest, touch removes all poison, disease, curse of 5th level or lower, blindness, deafness, and paralysis from one creature.

20.d: 1/long rest, choose life or death for a touched creature: full HP restored, or 10d10 necrotic (CON save halves).

3.d: A trained crow carries messages and small items and scouts on command; you see through its eyes within 100 ft.
Advantage on checks to source rare reagents, poisons, and cadavers in any settlement.

7.d: 1/long rest, a wheeling flock blinds enemies in a 20-ft radius for 1 minute (CON save each turn).

11.d: The guild's birds watch every district; ask the DM once per day where a named person was seen.

15.d: 1/long rest, when you drop to 0 HP, the flock carries you 60 ft to safety and you stabilize.

1.d: Bonus action: summon your bonded weapon to hand from anywhere; it counts as magical.
Enemies within 5 ft deal 1 less damage with weapon attacks (minimum 1).

2.d: Casts warlock-list spells as sworn oaths using CHA.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: 1/rest, spectral hands erupt in a 15-ft cube: creatures save (STR) or are restrained until your next turn.

7.d: Resistance to necrotic damage; you need half the usual food and sleep.

9.d: Reaction when an ally within 10 ft is hit: reduce the damage by your CHA mod + proficiency.

10.d: 1/long rest, when you drop to 0 HP, drop to 1 instead and gain temp HP equal to your level.

11.d: Your pact blade deals +1d8 necrotic; slain enemies cannot rise as undead unless you allow it.

13.d: Sepulchral Aura reaches 10 ft and also halves enemy healing within it.

14.d: 1/long rest, regain expended slot levels equal to your CHA modifier.

15.d: Immune to fear and charm; advantage against effects that would age, drain, or possess you.

17.d: 1/long rest, raise one slain foe as a loyal skeletal squire for 8 hours (uses its stats, obeys you).

18.d: Your pact blade's necrotic rises to +2d8; critical hits also frighten (WIS save negates).

20.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute you cannot drop below 1 HP.

3.d: 1/turn, when your pact blade hits, regain HP equal to your CHA modifier.
Advantage on CHA checks with mourners, undead, and those who serve death.

7.d: 1/rest, teleport 30 ft in a swirl of dead petals as a bonus action.

11.d: 1/long rest, a 20-ft radius garden of black roses for 1 minute: allies in it regain 1d6 HP per turn, enemies are slowed.

15.d: 1/long rest, when an ally within 30 ft dies, they instead drop to 1 HP and your next attack is an automatic critical if it hits.

1.d: When you attack, you may pay HP up to your proficiency bonus; add the same amount to the damage.
While below half HP, advantage on STR checks and +1 to attack rolls.

2.d: AC equals 10 + STR mod + WIS mod while unarmored.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: Advantage on saves against pain, fear, and exhaustion effects.

7.d: 1/rest, pay 5 HP: one attack this turn becomes an automatic hit if the roll would miss by 3 or less.

9.d: Resistance to slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

10.d: 1/long rest, when you drop to 0 HP, drop to 1 instead.

11.d: Mortified Flesh can pay up to twice your proficiency bonus in HP; the bonus damage is doubled while below half HP.

13.d: Reaction when an ally within 5 ft is hit: take the damage instead; it is halved for you.

14.d: Reduce all incoming weapon damage by your WIS modifier (minimum 1 damage taken).

15.d: While below one quarter HP, your attacks deal +2d6 damage and you cannot be frightened or charmed.

17.d: Martyr's Endurance recharges on a short rest; each use also grants temp HP equal to your level.

18.d: Regain WIS-mod HP at the start of each of your turns while below half HP and conscious.

20.d: 1/long rest, drop yourself to 1 HP: deal the HP paid as radiant damage divided among any creatures within 30 ft (DEX save halves).

3.d: Bonus action: chant for 1 minute; enemies within 10 ft have disadvantage on their first save against fear each turn.
Your unarmed strikes and flails deal +1d4 fire damage.

7.d: 1/rest, a scream: creatures in a 15-ft cone save (CON) or take 3d6 fire and are deafened for 1 minute.

11.d: Resistance to fire; when a creature hits you in melee, it takes your WIS mod in fire damage.

15.d: 1/long rest, wreathe yourself in gray flame for 1 minute: +2d8 fire on your attacks and immunity to fire.

1.d: Bonus action: an ally within 60 ft gains a d6 to add to one attack, check, or save; CHA-mod uses per long rest.
A low hum: one enemy within 30 ft subtracts 1d4 from its next attack roll (WIS save negates).

2.d: Casts bard-list spells as dirges using CHA; your voice is the instrument.

5.d: Requiem Inspiration die becomes a d8; regain all uses on a short rest.

6.d: 1/rest, all enemies within 30 ft that can hear you save (WIS) or deal half damage on their next attack.

7.d: Advantage on saves against fear and necrotic effects; you sense undead within 60 ft.

9.d: Your healing dirges add your CHA modifier again to HP restored.

10.d: Requiem Inspiration die becomes a d10.

11.d: 1/long rest, spectral voices join you for 1 minute: your dirges affect one additional target.

13.d: Dirge of Sorrow affects up to three enemies and the penalty applies to attacks and saves.

14.d: You and allies within 30 ft ignore difficult terrain and cannot be slowed while you sing.

15.d: Requiem Inspiration die becomes a d12.

17.d: 1/long rest, a killing note: one creature below 50 HP that can hear you saves (CON) or drops to 0.

18.d: Your dirges reach creatures that cannot hear (they resonate in the bone); deafness offers no protection.

20.d: When you roll initiative with no Requiem uses left, regain three; your inspiration dice explode (max rolls add another die).

3.d: A dying creature you sing over stabilizes automatically and is warded: it cannot be raised as undead.
When an ally within 30 ft drops to 0 HP, your next dirge this minute costs no slot if 2nd level or lower.

7.d: 1/rest, repeat the last dirge you cast this turn at a new target for free (3rd level or lower).

11.d: Allies within 30 ft gain resistance to necrotic damage while you sing.

15.d: 1/long rest, sing a fallen ally within a minute of death back to 1 HP without material components.

1.d: Your rats scout: learn the layout, occupants, and dangers of any building or sewer stretch within 100 ft after a minute.
1/turn, +1d6 damage against a creature that has not acted yet this combat or is surprised.

2.d: You know the sewer, cellar, and rooftop routes: in a settlement you can reach any point unseen with 10 minutes of travel.

5.d: 1/rest, a swarm floods a 15-ft cube: difficult terrain, and creatures inside save (DEX) or take 2d6 piercing and are distracted (disadvantage on their next attack).

6.d: Immune to disease; resistance to poison damage.

7.d: Dash, Disengage, or Hide as a bonus action.

9.d: Your Swarm Sense covers the whole district; ask the DM once per short rest what the rats have seen.

10.d: Beggars, urchins, and rat-folk treat you as royalty; one favor per day within their power.

11.d: Gutter Fighting damage rises to +2d6 and also applies to creatures adjacent to your swarm.

13.d: 1/rest, drop through a grate, crack, or shadow as a reaction when targeted; the attack misses and you move 15 ft.

14.d: Your rats carry messages, small items, and keys anywhere in the city within an hour.

15.d: Rat Tide has 2 uses per rest and its cube grows to 20 ft.

17.d: While your swarm is active, enemies within 10 ft of it cannot take reactions.

18.d: 1/long rest, summon a giant rat pack: four allied giant-rat-statted beasts serve for 10 minutes.

20.d: In any settlement: you cannot be tracked, always know who entered or left today, and your Rat Tide recharges on any rest.

3.d: A dire rat companion fights beside you (AC 13, HP 3 x level, bite 1d6 + DEX, obeys your commands).
You squeeze through any gap a rat could, at half speed.

7.d: You and your companion have advantage on attacks against a creature the other is adjacent to.

11.d: Your dire rat grows: HP 5 x level, bite 2d6 + DEX, and its bite forces a CON save against a weakening bite (disadvantage on STR checks 1 minute).

15.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute every enemy within 30 ft is swarmed: half speed, no reactions, and 1d6 piercing at the start of each of its turns.
